reviews.review_text: A love le place to stay and enjoy port fairy and sorroing of historal place witch there are quite a lot
Great location, clean amenities and friendly staff 🙂
Great caravan park, plenty of van spots or busses campers, friendly staff and great little shop to get snacks.
Very hospitable. Low peak so had choice of nice grassy sites. Nice clean and well maintained caravan park. Will definitely be back. Great location. Short walk to town.

Really good caravan park. Easy walking distance to the town, beach, a good playground and the local parkrun. The sites are good, with big trees/hedges providing shelter from the wind. Some sites did look a bit small though. Amenities are clean, showers are hot.
Lovely campsite. Good facilities. Hot showers. Right near the beach! Would stay again.
So good and great facilities.

Have been here several times over a number of years. In a great location for walks along the beach, easy walk into town and great grassy walks. Dog friendly. Bathroom facilities are excellent. Very clean and plenty of hot water and loo paper provided. On the down side, the free wi Fi is almost impossible to connect to and a lot of sites are not flat requiring lots of negotiation and out and requiring jacking up (a lot) one end of the van. The man at reception can be really rude and very unhelpful giving a bad  feeling to the start of your holiday. Best to deal with the women.
Perfect campsite. Thank you.
Great  place to  stay very helpful staff and clean amenities  Will be there next year  cheerz
Great size block. Great toilet block and the service was excellent.
Family friendly caravan park, great facilities.
Beautiful, quiet caravan park close to everything.  We love it.
Love this place
Too many visitor cars per site allowed so access and turning ability very restricted
Amazing park, so huge. The amenities are spottless and of plenty. Lots of walking trails
Great clean showers and bathrooms. Good size sites, close enough to town to walk into and close to the beach .
A small caravan park on the beach. Very helpful and knowledgeable staff. Very friendly. Excellent clean facilities.

4km walk round trip to griffin island lighthouse.
Nearby big playground for the kids
Toilet very clean.
Great place - huge sites with a great camp kitchen. Amenities are fantastic. Dated rubbish. They work and do what is expected.

We have been to this park for 11 years and the last 3 years we have had nothing but trouble with booking’s we get there and the site we booked they gave it to someone else and put us on a site up the back terrible site when we left we rebooked the site we were supposed to have when we got there gave it to someone else put us on a site to small for our setup on leaving we rebooked our original site and we just paid for it today and they rang up and said it’s not available on those days and there is no other sites available I will not be going back there and the 6 other sites our friends have booked will be cancelled and all the bad publicity I can send their way 😤😤😤😤
Love it great park and facilities

Worst manage caravan park I come across in Australia and I've been to plenty of great parks some peoples money isn't good enough.. don't let it stop you from visiting Port fairy best to see site on your own.. simply put

Great spacious park walking distance to town and wharf promenade, clean and well maintained ammenities, good hot showers, currently pay for 2 nights and get the third one free. Would have given 5 stars however for some inexplicable reason they allocated 3 vans close together in a 4 site space when the park with over 600 sites and an occupancy of around 10% w it’s surrounding sites vacant!

What a great park. We stayed for night on the 13th April ‘25. We had site 405 which was tucked away behind the hedges. It was so nice as we were out of the wind and so protected. Would have been nice to have stayed longer.
Great facilities and atmosphere

Nice place to park up for the night. Very family friendly, so not great if you want to party or aren’t fans of being woken up by kids in the morning.
But very clean showers and bathrooms, convenient cooking facilities and a safe and secure campground.
Fantastic place to stay, been going there for 22 years. I would highly recommend 😊

We stayed at Port Fairy on our way through to Melbourne Fantastic spot we stayed in one of the villas overlooking the beach it was very cosy and rooms were clean and staff very friendly and helpful ..
Southcombe Deluxe Villas are very comfortable, spacious, and gorgeous views of the park. Perfect place to relax and unwind.
Dropped in during the Port Fairy Folk Festival....very popular and friendly park next to the festival...
Deluxe cabins are first class, very spacious we with view of ocean
Great place to camp,tents,motor homes,caravans..lovely soft grass,cabins and ablution facilities plus camp kitchen,powered and non powered sites,close to the beach and walks on Griffith Island.

We recently slayed in Villa 3 at Southcombe  as two couples.
The experience could not be better,  all facilities were convenient, well set out and in excellent condition. Close to town and walking distance to Lighthouse etc.
Highly recommend to all.
Always a fantastic stay. Scenic, family friendly and only a short walk to Peasoup beach and town.
Sites are small, but nice grass surface. Some very private and some very open.
We stayed in one of the villas it was beautiful the views we great it was the best set up villa we have stayed in

We had a great time at this park with our group. The facility is clean and tidy, close to town and beautiful walks. It’s dog friendly. I do have a small suggestion to make dog owners life easier when using bathroom and kitchen facilities. Maybe a small hitch could be made outside of these spaces to attach the dog leads. I’ve seen them at some places and they are very useful. Thank you for sharing your space with us.
Very clean and modern amenities. Good sized sites and just a walk into town.

Jan was extremely busy with families, the park is great but kids riding around way to fast and around amenities which is teally unsafe with cars ( almost got knocked over a few times )
Overall I would go back out of season.as town and beaches very nice.
And water has a very weird thick taste ( buy  bottled for drinking)
Nice park plenty of space on sites

We have travelled extensively in our van, so are well equipped with what good caravan parks are like.
This hits all of the markers!

Clean toilets, big showers, food trucks, coffee vans, “private” beach, walking distance to the town centre, close to other activities- pool, tennis courts, walking tracks, basketball courts, football oval- we could go on.

We loved our powered site also having private hedges which give you your own sense of a “room”- ask for sites 50-60 powered for this! (There’s probably more, but maybe ask for the hedge powered sites).

We will absolutely be back!
It is a huge park and it was chocka.
Lovely quiet campground with all the facilities you could need in a great location next to the beach and close to town!

This was a quiet little spot
Check-in was interesting, no details were provided regarding anything. We did however work out the details that came were on sms. We did ask for a reasonably flat site as we had a motor home. Not so much and we ended up going back to discuss a few moments after checking in to find the office unattended. Then 10 minutes later closed. The park itself is lovely and well-located and well-set out.

Great place for a summer holiday. Also very convenient for Folkie Festival. Very clean amenities.  The camping ground is also very close to Pea Soup beach, which is a perfect beach for young children. Large sites. dog friendly.
Rude on phone after ringing up with a complaint

Went here for an in-state getaway, view of sunset was beautiful and rooms were clean and large enough to accommodate me and my 3 friends. Recommend a stay, but remember to bring a jacket as the wind is frosty.
Very friendly and accommodating staff. Lovely clean and well grassed park close to town. Very clean and tidy ammenities block. Highly recommend this park for a great Caravan stay.

The facilities are exceptionally clean and well maintained. The park is situated a very short walk from East & South beach, Griffiths island and the main street of Port Fairy.

A very relaxing place to stay.

We a group of approx 30 people stayed at the lodge! It was a great place to stay. It had 16 rooms, kitchen & LG toilet & shower blocks, lounge/TV area &  a LG games & TV area. A very comfortable stay! Driving through the park, there are LG amenitie areas in the park, powered & unpowered sites. It is also a clean, dog friendly park
This was a lovely and family friendly caravan park. Close to shops, beautiful walks and Griffith island.
Very friendly and helpful staff with reasonable rates
Wonderful park
Right next to the Port Fairy Folk Festival. Great stay and super music.
Great park, great location, nice and quiet in mid Feb but I could see it getting super busy. Showers are amazing!

Stayed here for 2 nights, nice and large park was quite busy while we stayed but the clientele are all super friendly and look out for each other!
Coin laundry was a bonus having somewhere to wash your clothes on a long road trip is much appreciated.
Rain head showers with good pressure and instantly warm. Facilities were all clean and well kept.
Only downfall was our site backed on to the sporting complex and there was cricket on but it's fine as they don't play into the night and you can walk to the beach or the nature reserve and enjoy some peace.
Walking distance from the main strip with cafes, iga and plenty of food. Also a short walk from the Wharf if you're after some fish and chips!
Great value good service in a town where demand exceeds availability
Nice park, clean an tidy, shower have good clearance, must be 6ft 6!
Very nice
Great place, very friendly campers, staff and dogs are welcome

On the plus side, Southcombe Park is well located with very easy access to bike and walking trails, Griffiths Island and some beaches. It's also a short walk into town. Also commendable are the spotlessly clean and well-maintained amenities; superb. And possibly the best hot showers you will find anywhere.
On the downside, as noted by another reviewer, the water has an odd taste, slightly antiseptic. We also ended up buying water to drink.
If you are camping in a tent be aware that many sites are open and the wind can be punishing and unpleasant. If you're in a van etc. or have a well-sheltered site then the wind will be much less of a challenge.

Park is in a great vicinity. Even though there is an indoor pool nearby for the town community, I felt that the park itself could have had an outside pool for park residents. We enjoyed our stay in Port Fairy.
Been coming here for many,many years. Love it.

Very tight smallish sites, but we did book at the last minute so understandable. Not impressed with paying $6.50 per child to use the swimming pool almost $40 and not counting the 2 adults it turned out an expensive trip.
Great campsite with fantastic facilities.

If you get a spot with a nice big hedge then it’s probably great. Unfortunately we had no shelter from the wind and it was pretty grim. Couldn’t put the annexe up, too windy.
Water tastes odd and we ended up buying bottles, not something I would normally do.
No wifi at this end of the camp, only got a weak signal when in the camp reception.
One of the ladies in the office was exceptionally rude, we were not the only people to notice that.
Toilets spotless and good hot showers. Easy stroll to the beach and the town.
Just be mindful of ending up in the wind tunnel :-) and book in early for a hedge

Previously we have used the unpowered tent sites at the campsite for the Port Fairy Festival, and we were planning to come down this year between Christmas and New Year.
We have always found the facilities at the campsite  to be very good and the location is spectacular. The campsite owners have always been friendly and helpful.
Unfortunately we have a family crisis to manage and we thought that we would just have to forgo the fees paid. After a warm and lovely chat with the campsite owner, we are overwhelmed by the generous gesture made. Thank you so so much.
We will return to your lovely campsite!
Happy New Year!

69 € l'emplacement pour un camping-car avec électricité. Les emplacements spacieux bien séparés par des haies. Le plus, c'est la petite île à côté à 10 minutes de marche, avec des wallabies facilement visibles. Accès au centre-ville 5 minutes à pied.
Spotlessly clean facilities, tidy, well maintained grounds, good shelter from the wind, highly recommended. Close to walks, wharf and beach, not far from town.

We've stayed elsewhere in Port fairy on another occasion, but we're so very glad we chose Southcombe this time, and will be our 'go to' for future. The Park is immaculately maintained, Sue was super friendly and informative, and we were so impressed with the privacy we had whilst there ... we did visit off-peak. The Park is surrounded by parkland and it's a short walk to the ocean. Very dog friendly. Perfect.
Beautiful park with extra friendly staff.

Just spent 3 nights here. Off season deal, pay for 2 get 3rd night free.$80 for 3 nights was awesome. Plenty of trees around each site for privacy. Excellent amenities, the camp kitchen had heaters,TV,  indoor free Bbqs plus everything else you would need. Very dog friendly. Staff were very welcoming. Highly recommended
A very tidy park. Clean amenities.  Enjoyed our stay.
Fantastic, clean facilities. Hot water tap for washing up, as well as the laundry, sinks, kitchen, really has everything
A great position right next to lake and surf. Very sheltered sites with tall pine trees giving great privacy and protection from the elements. Not to mention the showers and amenities were top class.
